events.title: Hamas Launches Unprecedented Attack on Israel
events.detail: Hamas and Palestinian Islamic Jihad fighters breach the Gaza fence at approximately 6:29 AM, launching a coordinated multi-point assault on southern Israel. Around 3,000 militants enter Israeli territory, attacking kibbutzim, towns, and military bases. Approximately 1,200 Israelis and foreign nationals are killed — the deadliest attack on Jews since the Holocaust — and 251 hostages are taken into Gaza.

events.title: Nova Music Festival Massacre — 364 Killed
events.detail: Hamas gunmen attack the Supernova Sukkot Gathering (Nova festival) near Kibbutz Re'im in the Negev desert. At least 364 young festival-goers are killed in one of the single deadliest sites of the attack. Dozens more are taken hostage. Survivors describe hours hiding in shelters and fields as gunmen hunted them.

events.title: Kibbutz Be'eri — 108 Residents Killed
events.detail: Hamas militants storm Kibbutz Be'eri, killing approximately 108 of its roughly 1,100 residents in house-to-house executions. More than 30 are taken hostage. Survivors describe militants shooting families in their safe rooms and burning houses with people inside. Be'eri suffers one of the highest per-capita death tolls of any community attacked on October 7.

events.title: Israel Declares State of War — Operation Swords of Iron
events.detail: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu convenes an emergency security cabinet meeting and declares a state of war. Israel launches Operation Swords of Iron (Mivtza Charvot Barzel), commencing intensive airstrikes across the Gaza Strip targeting Hamas military infrastructure. Defense Minister Yoav Gallant orders a 'complete siege' of Gaza: 'no electricity, no food, no water, no gas.'

events.title: 251 Hostages Taken to Gaza
events.detail: Hamas and other militant groups take 251 people hostage into Gaza, including Israeli citizens, soldiers, and foreign nationals from 25 countries. Hostages range in age from 9 months to 85 years. The hostage crisis becomes a central focus of Israeli war aims and subsequent negotiations, with families organizing public pressure campaigns worldwide.

events.title: Al-Ahli Hospital Explosion — Disputed Death Toll
events.detail: An explosion at Al-Ahli Arab Hospital in Gaza City kills hundreds of people sheltering there. Hamas and Palestinian health officials initially claim Israeli airstrikes killed 500 people; Israel and the United States assert a misfired Palestinian Islamic Jihad rocket caused the explosion. The incident triggers massive protests across the Arab world. A UN Commission of Inquiry later finds the explosion caused by a projectile launched from within Gaza, though the exact death toll remains contested at 100–471.

events.title: IDF Launches Ground Invasion of Northern Gaza
events.detail: The Israel Defense Forces launch a full-scale ground invasion of the Gaza Strip, beginning with northern Gaza. Infantry, armor, and engineering units advance into Gaza City from multiple directions. The IDF cuts off northern Gaza from the south, ordering the roughly 1.1 million residents of northern Gaza to evacuate southward — one of the largest mass displacement orders in recent history.

events.title: US Vetoes UN Ceasefire Resolution
events.detail: The United States vetoes a UN Security Council resolution calling for humanitarian pauses in Gaza. The US casts the sole veto, with Russia and China voting in favor and 10 other members also in favor. Washington argues the resolution failed to affirm Israel's right to self-defense. This becomes the first of multiple US vetoes on Gaza ceasefire resolutions through 2024.

events.title: Jabalia Refugee Camp Strike Kills Hundreds
events.detail: Israeli airstrikes hit the Jabalia refugee camp — the largest in Gaza — killing at least 126 people and wounding hundreds more. The IDF states it targeted Ibrahim Biari, a Hamas commander responsible for the October 7 attack, but acknowledges civilian casualties. The strike creates a massive crater and destroys surrounding apartment buildings, drawing widespread international condemnation.

events.title: Total Communications Blackout in Gaza
events.detail: Gaza experiences a near-total communications blackout as internet and telephone services collapse, coinciding with the launch of the ground invasion. The blackout lasts approximately 34 hours and severely hampers humanitarian operations, journalistic coverage, and coordination between Gaza's health system and the outside world. ICRC warns the blackout cuts off families from each other and from emergency services.

events.title: IDF Raids Al-Shifa Hospital Complex
events.detail: Israeli special forces and infantry enter Al-Shifa Hospital, Gaza's largest medical facility, claiming it sits atop Hamas's main command-and-control tunnel network. The IDF shows weapons, ammunition, and what it describes as tunnel infrastructure. Hamas and medical staff deny the hospital serves as a military base. WHO and MSF report the hospital is largely non-functional, with patients and staff trapped without power, water, or supplies.

events.title: First Ceasefire: Hamas Releases 50 Hostages for 150 Prisoners
events.detail: Qatar, Egypt, and the United States broker a four-day humanitarian ceasefire. Hamas agrees to release 50 hostages (women, children, and elderly) in exchange for Israel releasing 150 Palestinian prisoners. Aid convoys enter Gaza during the pause. The deal is subsequently extended by two days. In total, 105 hostages and 240 Palestinian prisoners are exchanged before the ceasefire collapses on December 1.

events.title: Ceasefire Collapses — Fighting Resumes
events.detail: The temporary ceasefire expires after Hamas accuses Israel of violating it by opening fire in Gaza. Israel says Hamas broke the truce by firing rockets toward southern Israel. Heavy fighting resumes across Gaza, particularly in northern Gaza and expanding toward Khan Younis and central Gaza. The international community urges renewed negotiations, but both sides blame each other for the breakdown.

events.title: IDF Declares Gaza City 'Under Control'
events.detail: Israeli forces announce they have encircled and largely taken control of northern Gaza, including most of Gaza City. Heavy fighting continues in the Shejaiya neighborhood and around the tunnels. The IDF discovers extensive tunnel networks beneath Gaza City, including under al-Shifa Hospital and in residential areas. Northern Gaza is largely reduced to rubble, with most residents having fled south.

events.title: South Africa Files Genocide Case Against Israel at ICJ
events.detail: South Africa files an application at the International Court of Justice (ICJ) under the Genocide Convention, accusing Israel of committing genocide against the Palestinian people in Gaza. The application cites statements by Israeli officials, the scale of civilian casualties, destruction of infrastructure, and the blocking of humanitarian aid. South Africa requests urgent provisional measures to halt military operations.

events.title: ICJ Orders Provisional Measures — Genocide Convention Applies
events.detail: The ICJ unanimously rules it has jurisdiction over the case and issues provisional measures. The court orders Israel to take all measures within its power to prevent acts that could fall under the Genocide Convention, ensure humanitarian aid reaches Gaza, preserve evidence, and report to the court within one month. The court stops short of ordering a ceasefire but the ruling is widely seen as a significant rebuke of Israeli conduct.

events.title: IDF Launches Major Offensive in Khan Younis
events.detail: Israeli forces shift the main axis of ground operations to Khan Younis in southern Gaza, where senior Hamas leaders including Yahya Sinwar are believed to be hiding. Intense fighting engulfs residential neighborhoods, forcing hundreds of thousands of additional displaced Palestinians into an ever-shrinking area around Rafah. The battle for Khan Younis lasts several months.

events.title: US, UK Suspend UNRWA Funding After Israeli Allegations
events.detail: The United States, United Kingdom, Australia, Canada, Germany, and other nations suspend funding to UNRWA after Israel alleges that 12 UNRWA staff participated in the October 7 attacks. UNRWA immediately dismisses the accused employees and pledges an investigation. The funding suspension, affecting the main humanitarian agency in Gaza, prompts warnings from UN officials that it could trigger catastrophic humanitarian collapse.

events.title: Flour Massacre — 112 Killed Waiting for Aid
events.detail: At least 112 Palestinians are killed and 760 wounded near the Kuwait Roundabout in Gaza City while waiting for aid convoys. Palestinian officials and witnesses say Israeli forces opened fire on the crowd; Israel says most casualties resulted from a stampede and that it fired warning shots to disperse the crowd. The incident, known as the 'Flour Massacre,' triggers international outrage and demands for an independent investigation.

events.title: IDF Strikes World Central Kitchen Convoy — 7 Aid Workers Killed
events.detail: Israeli airstrikes hit a convoy of three World Central Kitchen vehicles in Deir al-Balah, killing seven humanitarian aid workers from Australia, Poland, the UK, and a dual US-Canadian citizen. The convoy was coordinating its movement with the IDF prior to the strikes. The killing triggers a global outcry, prompts multiple countries to summon Israeli ambassadors, and leads to WCK temporarily suspending operations in Gaza. Israel fires two officers and reprimands others.

events.title: UN Security Council Passes First Gaza Ceasefire Resolution
events.detail: The UN Security Council passes Resolution 2728, calling for an immediate ceasefire in Gaza for the remainder of Ramadan, release of all hostages, and increased humanitarian access. The United States abstains rather than vetoing, a significant shift from its previous position. Israel criticizes the US abstention and briefly cancels a visit by Israeli officials to Washington. Israel says it does not consider itself bound by the resolution.

events.title: ICJ Orders Additional Measures — Gaza Famine Imminent
events.detail: The ICJ issues additional provisional measures, ordering Israel to ensure unimpeded access for humanitarian aid to Gaza and to enable the unhindered provision of urgently needed basic services and humanitarian assistance. The court notes the famine risk described by UN agencies and warns that Gaza faces 'an imminent catastrophic humanitarian disaster.' Israel disputes the characterization.

events.title: IDF Launches Rafah Ground Operation Despite International Warnings
events.detail: Israeli forces launch a ground operation in Rafah, at the southernmost tip of Gaza, where over 1.4 million displaced Palestinians had sheltered. The operation begins with the seizure of the Palestinian side of the Rafah border crossing with Egypt, cutting off a major entry point for humanitarian aid. The US, EU, and other allies had explicitly warned against a major Rafah operation without civilian protection plans.

events.title: ICJ Orders Israel to Halt Rafah Military Offensive
events.detail: The International Court of Justice orders Israel to immediately halt its military offensive in Rafah that 'may inflict on the Palestinian group in Gaza conditions of life that could bring about its physical destruction in whole or in part.' Israel states it will continue its military operations in compliance with international law. It is the first time the ICJ has ordered a state to halt an ongoing military operation.

events.title: ICC Prosecutor Seeks Arrest Warrants for Netanyahu, Gallant, and Hamas Leaders
events.detail: International Criminal Court Prosecutor Karim Khan announces he is seeking arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Defense Minister Yoav Gallant for war crimes and crimes against humanity, including using starvation as a method of warfare. He simultaneously seeks warrants for Hamas leaders Yahya Sinwar, Mohammed Deif, and Ismail Haniyeh for the October 7 attacks. Israel and the US condemn the move as outrageous.

events.title: US Pauses Delivery of Heavy Bombs to Israel Over Rafah Concerns
events.detail: The Biden administration pauses a shipment of 1,800 2,000-pound bombs and 1,700 500-pound bombs to Israel, citing concerns they could be used in densely populated Rafah. It marks the first time the US withholds weapons from Israel since the war began. The decision is partial and limited — other arms continue to flow — but signals growing US unease over the humanitarian situation. Israel condemns the move.

events.title: Spain, Ireland, and Norway Recognize Palestinian State
events.detail: Spain, Ireland, and Norway simultaneously announce recognition of Palestine as an independent state, effective May 28, 2024. The coordinated move brings the total number of countries recognizing Palestine to 144. Israel recalls its ambassadors from all three countries in protest. The recognition wave accelerates through 2024 and 2025, with additional European and Latin American nations following suit.

events.title: UN Security Council Adopts US-Proposed Ceasefire Framework
events.detail: The UN Security Council votes 14-0 (with Russia abstaining) to adopt a resolution endorsing President Biden's three-phase ceasefire proposal. Phase 1 calls for an immediate six-week ceasefire, withdrawal from populated areas, and release of some hostages including the elderly and sick. Phase 2 would end the war permanently. Phase 3 would begin multi-year Gaza reconstruction. Hamas calls it a promising framework but seeks guarantees of permanent ceasefire before agreeing.

events.title: Nuseirat Camp Operation — 270+ Palestinians Killed, 4 Hostages Freed
events.detail: Israeli forces conduct a daylight raid on the Nuseirat refugee camp in central Gaza to free four hostages (Noa Argamani, Almog Meir Jan, Andrey Kozlov, Shlomi Ziv). The operation results in at least 274 Palestinians killed and over 700 wounded, making it one of the deadliest single-day incidents of the war. Israel hails the operation as a heroic success; Palestinian and international observers decry the mass civilian casualties.

events.title: Hamas Political Chief Ismail Haniyeh Assassinated in Tehran
events.detail: Ismail Haniyeh, the head of the Hamas Political Bureau and the lead Hamas negotiator for ceasefire talks, is killed in an explosion at his residence in Tehran while attending the inauguration of Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian. Iran and Hamas blame Israel; Israel does not officially confirm or deny responsibility. Haniyeh's assassination deals a major blow to ceasefire negotiations and triggers warnings of regional escalation from Iran and Hezbollah.

events.title: IDF Strike Targets Hamas Military Chief Mohammed Deif
events.detail: Israel carries out a large airstrike on Khan Younis targeting Mohammed Deif, the elusive commander of Hamas's military wing (Izz ad-Din al-Qassam Brigades) and the primary planner of the October 7 attack. The strike kills at least 90 Palestinians. Israel announces weeks later that Deif was confirmed killed. Also killed is senior Hamas commander Rafa Salama. The strike is one of Israel's most significant targeted killings during the war.

events.title: ICC Issues Arrest Warrants for Netanyahu and Gallant
events.detail: The International Criminal Court issues arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and former Defense Minister Yoav Gallant for war crimes and crimes against humanity in Gaza, including the use of starvation as a method of warfare and attacks on civilians. All 124 ICC member states are obligated to arrest them if they enter their territory. Israel declares the ICC has no authority over it. The US expresses concern about the decision.

events.title: Yahya Sinwar Killed in IDF Operation in Rafah
events.detail: Yahya Sinwar, the supreme leader of Hamas and the principal architect of the October 7 attack, is killed during an IDF operation in the Tal al-Sultan area of Rafah. He is identified through DNA and fingerprint analysis. Sinwar's death ends the search for the most wanted figure on Israel's target list and removes Hamas's top decision-maker. Israel calls it a historic achievement; Hamas announces Mohammed Sinwar (his brother) as interim military leader.

events.title: Exploding Pagers Kill 12, Wound 2,800 Hezbollah Members in Lebanon
events.detail: Thousands of pagers used by Hezbollah members simultaneously explode across Lebanon, killing at least 12 people and wounding approximately 2,800. The next day, walkie-talkies explode, killing 20 more. Lebanon and Hezbollah blame Israel, which does not confirm responsibility. The operation is described as a remarkable intelligence achievement. The attacks accelerate Hezbollah-Israel hostilities that eventually lead to a Lebanon ceasefire in November 2024.

events.title: Israel-Hezbollah Ceasefire Takes Effect
events.detail: A US-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah takes effect in Lebanon, ending over two months of escalating exchange that saw Israel conduct major operations in southern Lebanon. The 60-day ceasefire calls for the withdrawal of Israeli forces from Lebanon and Hezbollah withdrawal north of the Litani River. While fighting in Gaza continues unabated, the Lebanon ceasefire relieves pressure on Israel's northern front.

events.title: IDF Returns to Al-Shifa Hospital — Second Major Operation
events.detail: Israeli forces conduct a second major operation at Al-Shifa Hospital, lasting nearly two weeks. The IDF says it kills or captures over 900 militants and finds significant weapons caches, including a command center. Palestinian medical officials and the UN say hundreds of patients and medical staff remain trapped without food or water. The hospital is left largely destroyed and non-functional.

events.title: Israeli Strike Ignites Tent Camp in Rafah — 45 Killed
events.detail: An Israeli airstrike on an area designated as a humanitarian zone in Tal al-Sultan, Rafah, strikes near a large tent encampment. The strike ignites fires that kill at least 45 displaced Palestinians. The IDF states it targeted senior Hamas commanders and that the fire was an unintended consequence; a Hamas statement denies senior commanders were present. The incident triggers global outcry and renewed calls for an immediate ceasefire.

events.title: Phase 1 Ceasefire Agreement Takes Effect
events.detail: A phased ceasefire agreement brokered by Qatar, Egypt, and the United States takes effect after being approved by the Israeli cabinet. Phase 1 provides for a 42-day ceasefire during which Hamas releases 33 hostages (women, elderly, and those with medical conditions) in exchange for Israel releasing approximately 1,890 Palestinian prisoners. Israeli forces partially withdraw from populated areas and aid flows increase dramatically. The deal marks the first significant pause in fighting since December 2023.

events.title: First Hostages Released — Gadi Mozes, Agam Berger, Romi Gonen
events.detail: In the first hostage release under the Phase 1 ceasefire, Hamas releases three female hostages — Romi Gonen, Emily Damari, and Doron Steinbrecher — in a choreographed handover in Gaza City. Simultaneously, Israel releases 90 Palestinian prisoners. Subsequent weeks see additional hostage-prisoner exchanges approximately every three to four days, with emotional reunions broadcast worldwide.

events.title: Phase 1 Extended — Phase 2 Negotiations Begin
events.detail: As Phase 1 of the ceasefire concludes, Israel and Hamas agree to extend the pause for an additional period while negotiating Phase 2, which would require Hamas to release remaining living hostages and Israel to permanently end the war. Negotiations are complicated by Israeli insistence on the right to resume operations and Hamas demands for a full Israeli withdrawal from Gaza. Multiple rounds of talks in Doha and Cairo follow.

events.title: Second Ceasefire Takes Effect — All Remaining Hostages Released
events.detail: A second ceasefire between Israel and Hamas takes effect on October 10, 2025, brokered again by Qatar, Egypt, and the United States. All 20 remaining living Israeli captives are released on October 13, 2025 in exchange for 250 Palestinian prisoners and 1,700 Palestinians who had 'disappeared' into Israeli detention. Israel also returns 28 bodies of deceased hostages for 360 Palestinian bodies. All hostages are accounted for by January 26, 2026.

events.title: Ceasefire Violated 1,620+ Times — Palestinians Continue to Die
events.detail: The October 2025 ceasefire is violated over 1,620 times through February 2026, including 560 civilian attacks, 749 bombardments, 79 raids, 232 demolitions, and 50 detentions. An additional 673+ Palestinians are killed and 1,799 injured during the ceasefire period. OCHA documents ongoing Israeli military activity despite the agreement, and aid flows are again restricted from late February 2026 due to crossing closures.

events.title: IDF Resumes Military Operations After Phase 2 Talks Stall
events.detail: After negotiations on Phase 2 of the ceasefire stall over disagreements on permanent war termination and Israeli troop withdrawal, Israel resumes military operations in Gaza. The IDF announces strikes on Hamas infrastructure across the Strip. Hamas accuses Israel of violating the ceasefire agreement. Mediators redouble diplomatic efforts to restore the truce. Humanitarian aid flows are again restricted.

events.title: Israel Blocks All Humanitarian Aid to Gaza
events.detail: Israel blocks all humanitarian aid from entering Gaza following the breakdown of Phase 2 ceasefire negotiations, cutting off the approximately 2 million Palestinians who depend on international aid. UN agencies warn of catastrophic famine risk. The US expresses concern but initially does not condition arms sales. Multiple countries and the UN Secretary-General condemn the blockade as a violation of international humanitarian law.

events.title: Israeli Law Banning UNRWA Operations Takes Effect
events.detail: Israeli legislation banning UNRWA from operating in Israeli territory and from coordinating with Israeli authorities takes effect, effectively ending the agency's ability to deliver aid through Israeli-controlled crossings. The UN Secretary-General calls it unprecedented and warns of catastrophic consequences for millions of Palestinians. Israel says UNRWA is infiltrated by Hamas; an independent review found systemic problems but did not substantiate all Israeli allegations.

events.title: ICJ Continues Hearings on Israeli Occupation — Broader Case
events.detail: Separate from the genocide case, the ICJ conducts hearings on the legality of Israel's prolonged occupation of Palestinian territories, following an advisory opinion request from the UN General Assembly. Over 50 countries participate. The court is expected to issue an advisory opinion that could further isolate Israel internationally and add legal pressure on states supporting Israeli occupation. The genocide case proceedings also continue with Israel submitting counter-memorials.

events.title: Trump Administration Takes Office — New US Policy Toward Gaza
events.detail: The Trump administration takes office in January 2025 and signals a significantly different approach to the Gaza conflict. President Trump proposes that the US take over Gaza and relocate Palestinians to Egypt and Jordan — a proposal rejected by virtually all regional governments and the Palestinian Authority. The administration presses Israel toward the Phase 1 ceasefire deal in January but later signals greater support for Israeli military operations.

events.title: Northern Gaza Declared in 'Full-blown Famine' by UN
events.detail: UN and humanitarian agencies declare northern Gaza facing famine conditions as food, water, fuel and medicine supplies collapse under the blockade and fighting. WFP warns that 576,000 people in Gaza face catastrophic food insecurity. Bakeries close as flour supplies run out; water desalination plants shut down without fuel.

events.title: IDF Arrests Kamal Adwan Hospital Director Dr. Hussam Abu Safiya
events.detail: Israeli forces raid Kamal Adwan Hospital in Beit Lahiya, arrest its director Dr. Hussam Abu Safiya, and detain dozens of medical staff. The hospital, the last functioning medical facility in northern Gaza, is left without leadership and forced to stop operations. WHO and international medical organizations demand his release. The arrest draws widespread international condemnation.

events.title: Pro-Palestinian Campus Protests Sweep US and European Universities
events.detail: Students at Columbia University establish an encampment on April 17, 2024 demanding divestment from Israel-linked companies, triggering a wave of similar protests at over 100 US universities and dozens of European institutions. Police arrest over 3,000 students. The protests reflect deep generational divisions over US support for Israel and spark major debates about academic freedom, antisemitism, and free speech.

events.title: IPC Declares Famine Conditions in North Gaza
events.detail: The Integrated Food Security Phase Classification (IPC) formally declares famine conditions in northern Gaza, the first famine declaration in the region in decades. The IPC finds that 1.1 million people — the entire population of northern Gaza — face catastrophic food insecurity. The finding triggers renewed international pressure for aid access and ceasefire.

events.title: Israeli Knesset Passes Laws Banning UNRWA
events.detail: The Israeli Knesset passes two laws banning UNRWA from operating within Israel and from coordinating with Israeli authorities, effectively ending UN relief operations through Israeli-controlled crossings. The laws take effect January 28, 2025. The UN, EU, US, and humanitarian organizations condemn the legislation as unprecedented and warn of catastrophic consequences for 2 million Palestinians dependent on UNRWA services.

events.title: Gaza: Most Dangerous Conflict for Aid Workers in History
events.detail: The UN Secretary-General declares Gaza the most dangerous place on earth to be an aid worker, with over 280 humanitarian workers killed — the highest toll in any single conflict tracked in UN history. UNRWA loses over 200 staff. Multiple IDF strikes hit clearly marked aid convoys and UN facilities. The UN Human Rights Council votes to investigate attacks on humanitarian workers.

events.title: Investigative Report Reveals IDF AI Targeting System 'Lavender'
events.detail: Israeli outlet +972 Magazine and Local Call publish an investigation based on Israeli intelligence sources revealing the IDF used an AI system called 'Lavender' to identify bombing targets, along with a system called 'Where's Daddy?' to track suspects to their homes for nighttime strikes. Sources say the system generated targeting lists with minimal human review. The IDF denied the characterization but the report triggers international debate about AI warfare ethics.

events.title: Hezbollah Secretary-General Hassan Nasrallah Killed in Beirut Airstrike
events.detail: Israeli airstrikes destroy Hezbollah's southern Beirut headquarters in the Dahieh neighborhood, killing Secretary-General Hassan Nasrallah — one of the most powerful figures in the Arab world for three decades. The strike also kills IRGC General Abbas Nilforoushan. Iran vows retaliation. Hezbollah names Naim Qassem as new Secretary-General. The killing represents Israel's most significant strike against the Axis of Resistance.

events.title: UN General Assembly Demands Immediate Ceasefire — 153-10 Vote
events.detail: The UN General Assembly passes a resolution calling for an immediate humanitarian ceasefire in Gaza by a vote of 153 to 10, with 23 abstentions. The US, Israel, and 8 others vote against. While non-binding, the vote reflects the overwhelming isolation of Israel and the US on the war. It is the largest UNGA vote against US-Israeli positions in modern history.

events.title: Netanyahu Dismisses Defense Minister Gallant, Appoints Katz
events.detail: Prime Minister Netanyahu dismisses Defense Minister Yoav Gallant and replaces him with Israel Katz, a political loyalist. Netanyahu cited differences over hostage negotiations and war strategy. Gallant had reportedly advocated for a hostage deal over military objectives. Mass protests erupt in Tel Aviv. Critics say the firing was designed to entrench the war coalition and block any deal.

events.title: Dutch Court Orders Halt to F-35 Parts Exports to Israel
events.detail: A Dutch appeals court orders the Netherlands to immediately stop exporting F-35 fighter jet parts to Israel, ruling there is a clear risk the parts could be used in serious violations of international humanitarian law in Gaza. The Netherlands had been a key transit hub for US-made F-35 components. The ruling is the first in Europe to legally halt arms exports and sets a precedent for similar cases in other countries.

events.title: Biden Pauses Delivery of 2,000-lb Bombs Amid Rafah Warnings
events.detail: The Biden administration pauses a shipment of 1,800 MK-84 2,000-pound bombs and 1,700 MK-82 500-pound bombs to Israel, the first time the US withholds weapons from Israel since the October 7 attack. The decision comes as Israel prepares a major Rafah operation. Israel condemns the move as 'inconceivable.' The pause is limited and temporary; other arms continue flowing. It marks a high point of US-Israel friction over civilian casualties.

events.title: West Bank Settler Violence and IDF Operations Escalate
events.detail: Violence in the West Bank escalates dramatically following October 7. Israeli settler attacks on Palestinian villages triple compared to 2022 levels, with multiple fatalities. IDF operations in Jenin, Nablus, and Tulkarm intensify. Palestinian Authority security forces are largely sidelined. By end of 2023, over 300 West Bank Palestinians are killed by Israeli forces or settlers — the highest toll since the Second Intifada.

events.title: Iran Launches Direct Attack on Israel — 300+ Drones and Missiles
events.detail: Iran launches its first-ever direct military attack on Israeli territory, firing over 300 drones, cruise missiles, and ballistic missiles toward Israel. The vast majority are intercepted by Israel's multi-layered air defense system with assistance from US, UK, French, and Jordanian forces. Only minor damage occurs on an IDF airbase in the Negev. Israel conducts a limited retaliatory strike on Iran days later, targeting near Isfahan. The exchange marks a dangerous escalation of the regional conflict.

events.title: Houthi Attacks on Red Sea Shipping in Solidarity with Gaza
events.detail: Yemen's Houthi movement begins attacking commercial ships and Israel-linked vessels in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden in November 2023, citing solidarity with Palestinians in Gaza. The attacks disrupt global trade, forcing major shipping companies to reroute around the Cape of Good Hope. The US and UK conduct military strikes on Houthi targets in Yemen beginning in January 2024. The attacks continue throughout 2024, causing billions in economic disruption globally.

events.title: Major IDF Operation in Jenin Refugee Camp
events.detail: Israeli forces launch Operation Iron Wall in the Jenin refugee camp in the West Bank, one of the largest West Bank military operations in decades. The operation involves hundreds of troops, drones, and armored vehicles and lasts multiple weeks. Dozens are killed, hundreds wounded, and over 40,000 Palestinians are displaced from Jenin. The PA temporarily suspends security coordination with Israel. International criticism is widespread.
